concerns.text: FR-012 (precipitation shift magnitude ΔP) has no anchoring to any user story. User Stories 1-3 do not mention reporting precipitation shift as a metric. This is an orphaned FR (silent scope creep). Either anchor to a story or remove.

FR-009 contains placeholder '[deferred] of records' for subsampling proportion. While [deferred] is sanctioned for planning docs, the functional behavior (what proportion to subsample) is undefined. This needs a concrete value or explicit reference to where it will be determined.
Template placeholder '[###-feature-name]' is unfilled. A converged spec must not carry unfilled template markers.
FR-009 still contains the placeholder '[deferred] of records' for the subsampling proportion, leaving the functional behavior undefined and thus not verifiable by a test.

concerns.location: spec.md:User Story 3 – Sensitivity Analysis of Sampling Effort
concerns.text: User Story 3 states the system will subsample '[deferred] of each species’ occurrence records', but the Acceptance Scenario 1 does not specify a valid range for this value. This makes the acceptance criteria untestable and the functionality undefined.
FR-007 and FR-012 both mention 'confidence interval' without specifying what type of confidence interval (e.g., 95%, 99%). This lack of specificity introduces ambiguity.

Acceptance Scenario 1 in User Story 2 states 'R² ≥ 0', which is trivially true and doesn't provide a meaningful acceptance criterion. A more useful criterion would be a minimum R² value or a significance level.

FR-009 contains '[deferred] of records' for the subsampling proportion. This leaves the functional behavior undefined — a test cannot verify what proportion to subsample. Per spec 020/023, [deferred] is sanctioned for empirical values, but the proportion itself must be specified for the requirement to be verifiable.
Acceptance Scenario 1 references '[deferred] of the records' for subsampling. Without a defined proportion, the Given/When/Then test cannot be executed deterministically.

Spec uses climate periods 1900-1950 and 1970-2020, but Constitution VI mandates WorldClim v2 layers (1970–2000 and 1991–2020). This drift from constitution data requirements invalidates reproducibility.
FR-009 still contains '[deferred] of records' placeholder for subsampling proportion. Functional behavior remains undefined and untestable per prior testability concern.
Template placeholder '[###-feature-name]' remains unfilled. Converged spec must not carry unfilled template markers.
Template placeholder '[DATE]' remains unfilled. Converged spec must not carry unfilled template markers.

ΔN (niche-shift magnitude) and ΔT (regional warming rate) are computed from the SAME occurrence data. FR-006 computes ΔT from 'all occurrence points of a species' while FR-005 computes ΔN from the same points. This creates a circular relationship: the predictor (ΔT) is not independent of the data used to compute the outcome (ΔN). Regional warming should be measured from an independent regional climate grid, not the species' own occurrence envelope.

Species-specific z-scoring (FR-005) makes ΔN incomparable across species. A species with narrow climate niche (small SD) will have larger standardized shift for the same absolute change. This violates the assumption that regression coefficients are comparable across taxonomic groups.

Linear regression with 10-15 species (per methodology sketch) has insufficient statistical power. With n<20, the slope estimate is highly unstable and confidence intervals will be extremely wide. This sample size cannot support the claimed inference about systematic variation across taxonomic groups.

Period definitions (1900-1950, 1970-2020) leave a 20-year gap (1950-1970) unaccounted for. The research question claims 'past century' coverage but the design has a temporal discontinuity that should be acknowledged.

new_concerns.text: FR-009 specifies '50% of records' for subsampling but User Story 3 still says '[deferred] of each species' occurrence records'. This creates an internal inconsistency between the requirement and its user story reference.

Acceptance Scenario 1 still contains '[deferred] of the records' for subsampling proportion. FR-009 now specifies 50%, but the test scenario remains undefined. This creates an inconsistency between FR and its test, making the scenario non-executable.

FR-009 now specifies '50% of records' which resolves the prior concern. However, this value should be consistent with User Story 3 Acceptance Scenario 1 which still shows [deferred]. The spec has internal inconsistency between FR and test scenario.

Statistical power concern (scientific_soundness-0de9327f) remains unresolved. FR-012 still specifies "minimum 10 species required" despite prior concern that n<20 yields unstable slope estimates with wide CIs. This sample size cannot support claims about systematic variation across taxonomic groups. The spec must either increase minimum species count or acknowledge power limitations.

Period gap concern (scientific_soundness-75419c5d) partially addressed but not resolved. Spec now uses 1970-2000 and 1991-2020 with a note about the 1950-1970 gap, but the research question still claims "past century" coverage. This temporal discontinuity should be explicitly acknowledged in the research question or the period definitions adjusted to match the claim.

new_concerns.text: User Story 3 Acceptance Scenario 1 still contains '[deferred] of the records' for the subsampling proportion, while FR-009 specifies '50%'. This creates an internal contradiction between the user story and the functional requirement, making the acceptance criteria untestable.

FR-012 mentions 'margin‑of‑error for the slope estimate' but does not specify the confidence level (e.g., 95%). FR-007 specifies 95% CI for the regression; FR-012 should be consistent with this to avoid ambiguity in the power analysis output.

Acceptance Scenario 1 still contains '[deferred] of the records' for subsampling proportion. The reviser claimed this was updated to '50%' but the actual spec text shows '[deferred]'. Without a defined proportion, the Given/When/Then test cannot be executed deterministically. Original concern ID: testability-1a5ffff8
